text: Rapid advancements in model scaling have enabled large language models (LLMs) to achieve remarkable zero-shot performance across a diverse array of natural language processing (NLP) tasks. The emergence of ChatGPT, in particular, has captivated the research community through its ability to generate sophisticated, human-like responses and iteratively self-correct based on conversational context. Despite its popularity, the extent to which ChatGPT functions as a truly versatile generalist across the broader NLP landscape remains largely unexplored. In this study, we provide a comprehensive empirical evaluation of ChatGPT’s zero-shot capabilities across 20 hallmark datasets spanning seven distinct task categories. Our findings highlight both the strengths and inherent boundaries of the current model; while ChatGPT excels in areas requiring complex reasoning, such as arithmetic logic, it continues to struggle with specialized tasks like sequence tagging. Supplementing our quantitative results, we offer a qualitative analysis of specific case studies to provide deeper insight into the model's behavior and limitations.
